prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Find the branch starting with `m4/` (run `git branch -a | grep 'm4/'`). There ma

risks_fired.evidence: `<repo-name>` angle-bracket placeholders on three cp-command lines inside the fence; cp silently no-ops if Claude substitutes the literal string, personal files don't copy into the worktree, M5/M6 compounding breaks without visible error.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Replace `<repo-name>` with a self-resolution instruction inside the fence: 'Determine the repo name from `basename $(pwd)` and substitute in all paths below.'

risks_fired.evidence: Four chained sub-steps (find branch, create worktree, copy files, report) with no in-fence suppressor and no body primer after the closing fence.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add a one-sentence body primer after the fence pointing the student to the shell-command output confirming the worktree path and which files copied across.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Re-run the task we just packaged. The reference, plan.md, and verifier are at the paths we wrote in this session.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Tell me what shipped, what didn't' — anti-softening covers the run itself, not the final report; RLHF softening frames partial failures as partial successes in the close-out summary M6 reads.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Extend report-back: 'Be specific about failures — don't soften them. The contrast with the previous run is what the next session reads.'

risks_fired.evidence: Self-summary of a long autonomous run; Claude inflates shipped count, undercounts verifier firings; summary is primary M6 diagnostic input.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add loose body callout after the fence: 'If the summary feels polished, ask Claude which artifact the verifier actually fired on, and whether the failure was resolved or logged in RUN-NOTES.md.'

maintainer_acceptance.note: Prompt 2 risks (niceness-tax, self-report-inflation) carried forward from cached trace — body callout after the fence and body-prose anti-softening address via accepted-with-mitigation per pedagogy rule 50. Prompt 1 file-preservation-gap re-evaluated 2026-05-14 (SHA changed after prompt edit): placeholders remain; preamble-before-action newly flagged. Prompt 1 acceptance requires maintainer review.
